topic: Introduction to JavaScript arrays, including declaration, zero-based indexing, the .length property, and iterating through elements using for loops and for...in loops.

concepts.name: array
concepts.explanation: A list of elements or 'a list of stuff,' like a shopping list or a recipe.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows you to store multiple values in a single variable instead of creating separate variables for every item.

concepts.name: zero-based indexing
concepts.explanation: The principle that counting starts at 0 rather than 1; the first item in an array is at index 0.
concepts.why_it_matters: If you try to access the first item using index 1, you'll actually get the second item, leading to logic errors.

concepts.name: .length property
concepts.explanation: A property of the array object that tells you how many elements are inside it.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows you to write loops that automatically adjust to the size of your list, making code more flexible.

concepts.name: for loop (iteration)
concepts.explanation: A way to repeat an action for every item in the array by using a counter (like 'y') that goes from 0 up to the length of the array.
concepts.why_it_matters: It automates repetitive tasks, like drawing every item in a list onto the canvas.

concepts.name: for...in loop
concepts.explanation: A more concise, shorthand way to loop specifically over the elements of an array.
concepts.why_it_matters: It provides a cleaner, more readable syntax when you only care about the elements themselves.

code_examples.demonstrates: How to declare an array with mixed data types (strings and numbers) using square brackets.
code_examples.common_issue: Using curly braces {} instead of square brackets [] which would create an object instead.

code_examples.demonstrates: How to access a specific element in an array using its index inside square brackets.
code_examples.common_issue: Using the wrong index (e.g., using 1 to get the first item) or forgetting the brackets.

code_examples.demonstrates: Using a standard for loop to iterate through an array and draw each element vertically.
code_examples.common_issue: Using `y <= arr.length` instead of `y < arr.length`, which causes an error because the index goes one step too far.

code_examples.demonstrates: Using a for...in loop to iterate through an array.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting that the variable in a for...in loop represents the index/key, not the value itself.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The loop tries to access an index that doesn't exist (e.g., trying to find the 4th item in a 4-item list).
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use the 'less than' operator (`<`) so the loop stops before it hits the length value.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Items in the list are overlapping or starting at the very top of the screen.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: The loop variable is being used directly as a coordinate without an offset (e.g., using `y` instead of `50 + y * 100`).
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Add a starting offset to the coordinate calculation.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The loop is printing the index number (0, 1, 2...) instead of the actual words in the list.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Referencing the loop variable directly (e.g., `text(el, ...)` instead of `text(arr[el], ...)`).
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use the index to look up the value in the array: `arr[el]`.

key_moments.description: The instructor provides a mnemonic: 'braces create an object, square brackets create an array'.
Introduction of zero-based indexing; warns that the first element is actually index 0.
Showing how to use `.length` to make loops dynamic.
Moving from the standard for loop to the more concise 'for...in' syntax.

prerequisites: Basic p5.js setup() and draw() functions
student_questions: How do I make a list of things?
Why is my first item index 0 and not 1?
How do I get the number of items in my list automatically?
Why is my loop going past the end of my array and breaking?
How do I draw everything in a list one after another?
What is the difference between {} and [] when making variables?
How do I use a loop to print all my array items on the screen?
Why does my for...in loop only show numbers instead of my text?
How do I make a list that can grow or change size?
